title: Temporary import of the goods registered in the inventory stock by oral declaration from the Free Zones
bodyHtml:
The declaration for the temporary importation and re-exportation of goods registered in the inventory stock of companies established in free zones for repair and maintenance purposes within the scope of the inward processing regime can be made by oral declaration within the scope of Article 171, paragraph 4 (c) of the Customs Regulations.

A customs circular numbered 2023/9 was published regarding how the procedures related to oral declaration will be carried out If it is desired to benefit from the inward processing regime within the scope of the relevant article.

If a company established in a free zone wants to temporarily import  certain goods  registered as an inventory stock  in the free zone processing form (SBIF) , for repair and maintenance purposes under the inward processing regime with an oral declaration, no separate inward processing permit will be requested from the importer.

All procedures related to the permit will be carried out through the oral declaration form arranged in electronic environment. In this context, the permit for the repair and maintenance process to be carried out can be given to companies located in the Turkish Customs Zone upon the request of the person who carries out or has the process done, as well as upon the request of the company located in the free zone, in accordance with the third paragraph of Article 110 of the Customs Law No. 4458 (it is possible to grant permission to persons located outside the Turkish Customs Zone for non-commercial temporary importation purposes). When the permit is obtained by the free zone operator company, the information of the firm that will perform the repair and maintenance process must be recorded in the "explanations" field of the oral declaration.

The guarantee for the operations to be carried out within the scope of the inward processing regime can also be provided by the free zone operator as a comprehensive guarantee.

If the guarantee is provided within the scope of the collective guarantee to be provided by the relevant free zone operator, the company / companies benefiting from the guarantee provided by the operator will be notified to the relevant Customs Directorate by the operator. Thus, no separate guarantee will be requested from the firms that have been notified to the relevant Customs Directorate in this way, other than the guarantee provided by the operator.

For oral declaration procedures related to the importation of goods to be repaired and maintained from the free zone and their re-export to the free zone, please click on circular numbered 2023/9.
